meaning: n.(因犯罪.过失.违约等而)丧失的东西,没收物,罚款vt.没收,丧失adj.丧失了的
examples: That is the forfeit he must pay.
If you cancel your flight, you will forfeit your deposit.
Do you think that they would forfeit profit in the name of safety?
The implication being that they will forfeit co-operation with Russia for a long time ahead.
If they continued to earn income, they would forfeit part of their retirement benefits.
